handoff_goal: Specify a unified Workflows feature that adds Settings workflow template management and Chat composer workflow entry in the current project.

current_project_roles.scope: Desktop Settings UI, server workflow template registry/API, desktop session creation flow, chat composer workflow entry
current_project_roles.evidence_source: user request plus live repository reads
current_project_roles.notes: Requested feature belongs in the active repository.

target_project_roles.scope: Add or adjust desktop UI/API behavior for workflow template management and workflow session creation
target_project_roles.evidence_source: user request plus live repository reads

target_paths_still_to_verify: Tests and fixtures adjacent to changed server and desktop files
Exact API/client surfaces chosen during planning for linked workflow session creation

source_evidence.claim: Registry owns workflow template loading/writing, built-in agent-development, user workflows.json, validation, unknown-field preservation, and builtin shadow rejection.
Current workflow template API is list-only and workflow metadata is created during session creation when workflow is present.
Workflow phase fields include instructions, requested model, skill declarations, required artifacts, completion criteria, transition authority, action policy, and phase prompt.
Runtime assembles active phase context from phase instructions, structured prompt, action policy, artifacts, completion criteria, skills, and model info.
Current launch controls load workflow templates and send selected workflow template metadata only during new session creation.
Workflow status and controls appear when session workflow metadata exists.
/compact clears conversation history but keeps a summary in context, with optional summarization instructions.
Product requirements, standard workflow model, UI sketches, and acceptance signals are recorded.
Selected technical direction and consequence obligations are recorded.

settings_workflows_management: Settings tab or item labeled Workflows
List built-in and user workflow templates
Show source, phase count, validity/startability, and invalid diagnostics
CRUD for global user-level workflow templates
Built-ins read-only/protected and copyable into user templates
JSON import/export with preview and selected import
Import conflicts auto-rename by default instead of overwriting
Import validates before write and cannot shadow built-in ids
standard_workflow_contract: Workflows are staged execution contracts, not prompt collections
Each phase defines identity, role/type, instructions, objective, intake, output artifact, handoff, execution rules, completion criteria, transition authority, and recovery notes where relevant
Every phase requires explicit output artifact and handoff contract
Advanced phase fields may include handoff intake, required output sections, completion/stop rules, required artifacts, action policy, requested model, and skill guidance
chat_workflows_entry: Selecting Workflows opens a workflow-template dialog
Empty/new sessions reuse current launch flow
Non-empty chats ask context strategy: inherit, summarize with compact-style semantics, or clear
Inherit/summarize create linked new workflow session carrying chosen context
Original normal chat is not mutated in place

non_goals: Do not rename or change the existing built-in workflow identity
Do not edit built-in templates in place
Do not add project-level workflow template storage or merge precedence in the first version
Do not mutate existing workflow session snapshots when source templates are edited
Do not model this as a clone of an unconfirmed Claude Code /workflow feature

soft_unknowns.claim: Exact detailed template schema editor versus optional raw JSON editing depth.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if implementation scope cannot fit the selected UX.

soft_unknowns.claim: Whether Settings exposes a shortcut from invalid template warnings in session creation.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if discoverability of invalid templates is a core acceptance criterion.

soft_unknowns.claim: Internal API shape for linked workflow session creation and compact-style summary carry-over.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if proposed API cannot preserve CA-005.

settled_decisions: Use Workflows as the user-facing label
Global user-level custom templates only for first version
JSON import/export with selected import and auto-rename conflicts
Workflow phases require output artifact and handoff contract
Chat entry uses composer plus menu
Non-empty chats require explicit context handling
Inherited/summarized context starts a linked new workflow session
recommended_sequence: Specify server-side template mutation and import/export contract
Specify desktop Settings Workflows UI and validation states
Specify chat composer Workflows dialog and linked-session behavior
dependencies: Registry write behavior must preserve unknown fields
Linked workflow session behavior must not mutate original normal chat
Summary path must map to /compact-style semantics or equivalent compaction service behavior
deferred_scope: Retroactive workflow session migration after template edits
Standalone clone of an unverified Claude Code /workflow feature
reopen_conditions: Direct built-in edits enter the design
Lossy workflows.json write behavior enters the design
Prompt-only phase authoring enters the design
Normal chats are converted in place without explicit linked-session behavior
Existing workflow session snapshots are retroactively mutated

confirmed_ui_decisions: Chat plus menu label is Workflows
Phase editor uses default/common section plus advanced collapsed section
Chat context dialog offers inherit, summarize, and clear choices
deferred_ui_decisions: Whether invalid template warnings link directly to Settings
interaction_expectations: Import preview with selectable workflow entries
Non-empty chat context handling before linked workflow session creation
state_requirements: Missing user config keeps built-in startable
Existing normal chats remain unchanged after linked workflow start
accessibility_expectations: Use standard buttons, dialogs, forms, validation messages, and keyboard-accessible controls
ui_discussion.ui_sketch_summary: Settings Workflows split list/editor sketch and Chat plus-menu workflow selection sketch.

state_behavior_matrix.behavior: Built-in workflow remains visible and startable.
Built-in remains startable; invalid issue is visible; writes must not silently destroy user content.
Visible and startable for future sessions.
Not startable; issue visible in Settings and creation surfaces.
Read-only unless duplicated into a user template.
Continues from snapshot; source template edits do not mutate it.
Can start Workflow in current launch flow.
Asks context strategy, then creates linked workflow session; original chat remains unchanged.
Remains workflow and shows workflow status/controls.
Must not allow unsafe workflow start/conversion unless explicitly designed and tested.

dependency_impact_table.impact: Desktop Settings UI, chat composer UI, sessions API client, server router, workflow template registry service.
Workflow runtime, workflow session state service, session list/detail metadata, tests/fixtures.
cc-jiangxia config directory, registry cache, workflow session snapshots.
Unknown fields in user config, built-in id rules, linear-only workflow constraints, API response shape, transcript persistence, workflow metadata.

recovery_and_validation_contract: Do not mutate protected Claude files outside cc-jiangxia-owned workflow config
Keep built-ins startable when user config is missing or invalid
Reset registry cache after successful writes
Reject built-in shadowing, duplicate ids, missing required phase contracts, and unsupported branching/loop/parallel/nested workflow definitions
Existing workflow sessions must continue from snapshots
Linked workflow session creation must be explicit, recoverable, and tested
Summarize path must reuse /compact semantics or an equivalent internal compaction path

coverage_gaps.claim: Import conflict policy resolved by user confirmation.
coverage_gaps.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if import overwrites by default.

coverage_gaps.claim: Exact UI depth for schema editing versus optional raw JSON affordances remains soft.
coverage_gaps.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if implementation scope cannot fit the selected UX.

coverage_gaps.claim: Exact internal API mechanism for linked workflow session creation and compact-style summary carry-over remains downstream design detail.
coverage_gaps.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if proposed API cannot preserve CA-005.

obligations.claim: Future implementation must keep built-in templates startable even when user workflow config is missing, malformed, or contains invalid templates.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Stop and reopen if a proposed design makes invalid user config block the built-in template.

obligations.claim: Future implementation must preserve unknown fields in cc-jiangxia-owned workflows.json for any write path.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Stop and reopen if write behavior rewrites the file from a lossy normalized model.

obligations.claim: Future implementation must treat built-in templates as protected from direct mutation and prevent user templates from shadowing built-in ids.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Stop and reopen if UI/API permits editing built-in templates in place or saving a user template with agent-development.

obligations.claim: Future implementation must not mutate existing workflow session snapshots when source templates are edited; changes apply to future sessions unless an explicit migration workflow is separately specified.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Stop and reopen if requirements imply retroactive mutation of running or completed sessions.

obligations.claim: Chat-page workflow selection must not silently convert existing normal chats into workflow sessions or silently discard context. Non-empty chats must require explicit user confirmation and one of: inherit context, summarize context with compact-style semantics, or clear context. Inherit/summarize create a linked new workflow session carrying the chosen context instead of mutating the original chat in place.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Stop and reopen if a design enters Workflows from a non-empty chat without explicit user confirmation, context handling selection, and linked-session behavior for inherited/summarized context.

obligations.claim: Custom workflow authoring must preserve required phase output artifacts and required phase handoff contracts as first-class schema, not as unstructured prompt text only.
obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Stop and reopen if a design cannot validate or display each phase's intake, output, handoff, completion, and transition contract.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Add a user-facing Workflows surface for managing workflow templates and starting workflow sessions from chat.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: sp-specify must express Settings management and Chat entry as one unified feature.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if scope drops either Settings management or Chat entry without user confirmation.

must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Settings and Chat labels should say Workflows.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if downstream naming switches to another product label.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: First-version custom workflow templates are global user-level templates only.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Do not add project-level template storage or precedence rules in this feature.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if project-level templates enter scope.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Built-in templates are read-only/protected and user customization happens by copying built-ins into user templates.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: UI/API must prevent direct built-in mutation and support copy-to-user-template behavior.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if built-ins become editable in place.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Settings management uses CRUD for user templates with a schema-aware form.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify create, duplicate/copy, edit, delete, validate, and save behavior.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if management becomes view-only or raw JSON-only.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: JSON import/export is in scope, import preview lets users choose workflows, and conflicts default to automatic rename rather than overwrite.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify export, import preview, validation, selection, auto-rename, and no-overwrite behavior.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if import silently overwrites or does not let users choose imported workflows.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Workflows are staged execution contracts, not prompt collections.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify workflow/phase schema around responsibility, intake, work, output, handoff, transition, and recovery.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if custom workflow authoring becomes prompt-only.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Every phase must require an explicit output artifact and handoff contract.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Validation and UI must require these fields.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if phases can save without output/handoff definitions.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Phase editor shows common fields first and advanced schema fields collapsed.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify default and advanced phase editor sections.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if UI exposes all fields flat without a usability decision.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Chat page exposes Workflows from the composer plus menu and opens a workflow selection dialog.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify plus-menu item, dialog, template selection, invalid warnings, and start action.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if entry point moves away from composer plus menu without user confirmation.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Non-empty chats require explicit context handling: inherit, summarize with compact-style semantics, or clear.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify confirmation dialog and three context strategy behaviors.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if non-empty chats enter Workflows without explicit context choice.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Inherit/summarize from a non-empty normal chat creates a linked new workflow session and does not mutate the original chat in place.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify linked session creation, provenance, navigation, and original-chat preservation.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if design converts original normal chats in place.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Existing workflow session snapshots must not be mutated when source templates are edited.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify template edits as future-session behavior.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if requirements imply retroactive mutation of running/completed workflow sessions.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Official Claude Code docs did not provide a stable standalone /workflow feature contract; use Skills/Commands as inspiration only.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Do not anchor requirements to an unverified external /workflow feature.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if a new external feature is used as a hard requirement without fresh evidence.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: No hard unknowns remain for handoff; soft UI/API details can be resolved downstream.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Carry soft unknowns but do not block specification on them.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if a soft unknown becomes a product contradiction.
